#  Retrieve an RFI from the Recycle Bin 
##  Background 
After you send an RFI to the Recycle Bin, you can retrieve it at a later time using the steps below.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * **Prerequisites:**
    * The RFI that you want to retrieve must be in the Recycle Bin. See [Delete an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/delete-an-rfi).
  * **Limitations:**
    * To give project teams the ability to restore any deleted item, the Recycle Bin cannot be emptied.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Click **Recycle Bin** at the top of the RFIs page.
  3. Locate the desired RFI and click **View**.
  4. Click **Retrieve**.


## See Also
  * [What is the RFI Manager role?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-rfi-manager-role)
  * [What is Procore's Recycle Bin?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-procores-recycle-bin)
  * [Delete an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/delete-an-rfi)
title: Retrieve an RFI from the Recycle Bin
description: How to retrieve an RFI from the RFI recycle bin.
